title: Turtle Soup (false-break fade) platform implementation: ~60% winners across FX pairs, GBP/USD 60.5%
claim: Fading an N-period extreme touch (buy at 5-period low, sell at 5-period high) with an EMA trend-direction filter and 3x ATR(20) stop produces around 60% winning trades on major FX pairs (GBP/USD 60.5%) in the vendor's backtests.

source.label: WH SelfInvest — free trading strategy no. 38: Turtle Soup
source.quote: A buy signal is given when the market price hits a 5-period low. A short sell signal is given when the market price hits a 5-period high. ... The stop is calculated by NanoTrader as 3x the 20-period ATR ... The percentage of winning trades is around 60% for all currency pairs. For the forex pair GBP/USD the percentage is, for example, 60,5%.

rule_formal: Long: price touches min(low, 5 periods) AND fastEMA > slowEMA. Short: price touches max(high, 5 periods) AND fastEMA < slowEMA. Time filter: signals only 06:00-22:00. Stop: 3 * ATR(20). Target: long exits at 5-period high (short at 5-period low).

numbers.effect: win rate ~60% across currency pairs; GBP/USD 60.5%
numbers.sample: vendor backtests on EUR/USD, EUR/GBP, EUR/CHF, EUR/JPY, GBP/USD; n trades and period not stated in text
numbers.caveats: vendor/platform marketing page; net return and profit factor not text-disclosed; wide stop (3x ATR20) vs near target (5-period extreme) means 60% win says nothing about expectancy; original Raschke Turtle Soup uses 20-day extremes, this is a 5-period variant

mechanism: Breakout traders' stops cluster just beyond the N-period extreme; a touch-and-fail forces the freshly entered breakout crowd to liquidate back through the level, fueling the fade.
relevance: Q1 + Q4 direct (formalizes sweep-vs-break as: extreme touched but trend filter opposes continuation -> fade; gives a concrete counter-hypothesis to test against our breakout entries at Asian high/low)

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — N-period extreme and EMA state known at touch

overlap_risk: EMA direction filter overlaps our slope filter (slope percentile falsified, but slope sign is in the live system); extreme-touch component is orthogonal to all five falsified variables
